content.problemStatement:
Chef has an integer NNN and he wants to generate a matrix MMM with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN). He thinks that MMM would be delicious if:


Each element of this matrix is an integer between 111 and N2N^2N2 inclusive.
All the elements of the matrix are pairwise distinct.
For each square submatrix containing cells in rows rrr through r+ar+ar+a and in columns ccc through c+ac+ac+a (inclusive) for some valid integers rrr, ccc and a‚Č•0a \ge 0a‚Č•0:

Mr,c+Mr+a,c+aM_{r,c} + M_{r+a,c+a}Mr,c‚Äč+Mr+a,c+a‚Äč is even
Mr,c+a+Mr+a,cM_{r,c+a} + M_{r+a,c}Mr,c+a‚Äč+Mr+a,c‚Äč is even


Can you help Chef generate a delicious matrix? It can be proved that a solution always exists. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print NNN lines describing a delicious matrix MMM. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain NNN space-separated integers Mi,1,Mi,2,‚Ä¶,Mi,NM_{i,1}, M_{i,2}, \ldots, M_{i,N}Mi,1‚Äč,Mi,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Mi,N‚Äč.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The matrix MMM has five square submatrices. Four of them ([1][1][1], [2][2][2], [4][4][4], [3][3][3]) have a=0a=0a=0, so they obviously satisfy all conditions. The last square submatrix is the whole matrix MMM, with r=c=a=1r=c=a=1r=c=a=1, and we can see that M1,1+M2,2=1+3=4M_{1,1} + M_{2,2} = 1 + 3 = 4M1,1‚Äč+M2,2‚Äč=1+3=4 and M1,2+M2,1=2+4=6M_{1,2} + M_{2,1} = 2 + 4 = 6M1,2‚Äč+M2,1‚Äč=2+4=6 are both even.
